title: I need the official track listing for the S2 Works
display_title: I need the official track listing for the S2 Works
wikitext:
[]: I need the official track listing for the S2 Works             Quote Post by FAII » Wed Jul 01, 2009 3:06 pm So I'm working on putting all NGE music (all albums & singles) on my iPod and since I'm extremely pedantic when it comes to my labeling, I want to label the tracks as they appear in the track listing for their respective albums. I've looked for hours without finding scans of the official track listing for any of the S2 WORKS CDs so now I turn to you guys. Some of you must have an (opened) copy of the S2 WORKS to check for me :P. Different sources give conflicting information. This is what I've dug up: Reichu's old site: 09. OP-2, D-type [Good, or Don't Be] Japanese Wikipedia: 09. OP-2 Dタイプ 「Good, or Don't Be.」 Japanese Wikipedia looks reliable seeing as how the Japanese prefer 」 over ] and the fact that it's spot on for the other albums I've tagged insofar (NGEI-III, DEATH, THE END OF EVANGELION, DECADE). However, there are a few... anomalies on the Japanese Wikipedia listing, such as: 01. "OP-1 ストリングス" Every track with kanji in it is surrounded by quotation marks. Why? Is it really like this on the official track listing (back covers? CD booklet?). I'd appreciate it tremendously if someone who owns the box could check it out for me. The best case scenario would be an actual scan or photo (I don't care about the quality as long as I can read the writing), but words will be enough. I just want to know how the tracks are officially listed :P. Thanks in advance, guys.

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Wed Jul 01, 2009 3:34 pm http://wiki.evageeks.org/S2_Works Hope this helps.

[]:             Quote Post by FAII » Thu Jul 02, 2009 12:57 am Sailor Star Dust wrote: http://wiki.evageeks.org/S2_Works Hope this helps.

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Thu Jul 02, 2009 8:38 am Well then you don't want a track listing, you want a scan. The only person I know who definitely has the S2 works is Rachel. Private message her and ask her if she can scan it for you. Be sure to include "pretty please with a cherry on top". http://forum.evageeks.org/privmsg.php?mode=post&u=150

[]: Re: I need the official track listing for the S2 Works             Quote Post by Oedipusfoot » Thu Jul 02, 2009 9:14 am FAII wrote: I've looked for hours without finding scans of the official track listing for any of the S2 WORKS CDs so now I turn to you guys. Some of you must have an (opened) copy of the S2 WORKS to check for me :P. Different sources give conflicting information. This is what I've dug up: Reichu's old site: 09. OP-2, D-type [Good, or Don't Be] Japanese Wikipedia: 09. OP-2 Dタイプ 「Good, or Don't Be.」

[]: Re: I need the official track listing for the S2 Works             Quote Post by FAII » Fri Jul 03, 2009 6:01 am The Eva Monkey wrote: Well then you don't want a track listing, you want a scan. The only person I know who definitely has the S2 works is Rachel. Private message her and ask her if she can scan it for you. Be sure to include "pretty please with a cherry on top". http://forum.evageeks.org/privmsg.php?mode=post&u=150

[]: Re: I need the official track listing for the S2 Works             Quote Post by Oedipusfoot » Fri Jul 03, 2009 7:16 am FAII wrote: So, for example, this is what the track listing should be: Disc 1, track 5: 05. "A-3 リズムのみ" Disc 5, Track 9: 09. OP-2 D-Type (no commas, "Type" in English and not in Japanese)

[]:             Quote Post by FAII » Sun Jul 05, 2009 2:51 am Thank you so much! So, just to make sure, western brackets (full brackets; []) instead of Japanese ones? That's the only thing that wasn't entirely clear, though prety heavily implied", in your previous post.

[]:             Quote Post by Oedipusfoot » Sun Jul 05, 2009 5:06 am FAII wrote: So, just to make sure, western brackets (full brackets; []) instead of Japanese ones?

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Sun Jul 05, 2009 2:21 pm I do have scans somewhere, but, uh... I'll upload them once I reinstate the Internet on my main computer. ...Yeah. And then I'll fix the abysmally inaccurate track listing that's on the Wiki, if it's still there.

[]:             Quote Post by FAII » Tue Jul 07, 2009 10:02 am Reichu wrote: I do have scans somewhere, but, uh... I'll upload them once I reinstate the Internet on my main computer. ...Yeah. And then I'll fix the abysmally inaccurate track listing that's on the Wiki, if it's still there.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Mon Aug 10, 2009 9:57 am Any time Reichu says she'll do something, there's always a chance she will. It's just a matter of when. Sorry these aren't the pages that list nothing but the tracks themselves, but picking out the track names from the rest should be simple enough. http://www.evageeks.org/images/misc/s2_p05.gif http://www.evageeks.org/images/misc/s2_p06-07.gif http://www.evageeks.org/images/misc/s2_p08-09.gif http://www.evageeks.org/images/misc/s2_p10-11.gif I do have crappy old translations for all these pages lying around, but they need a good comb-over, and my Jenga tower is pretty high at the moment.

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Mon Aug 10, 2009 10:12 am 404 with the third scan (page 8 & 9). Thanks for these!!

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Tue Aug 11, 2009 7:29 am I fixed the file name, and bloody copy-pasted it over, though the website keeps telling me it's 404. <_< Maybe it will be less retarded for somebody else.

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Tue Aug 11, 2009 11:32 am Worked fine for me just now

[]:             Quote Post by FAII » Wed Aug 19, 2009 9:46 am So I'm finally labeling my S2 Works MP3s and using the scans, I've able to list the songs exactly as they appear in the booklet. The only problem is that no Japanese site seems to list certain kanji which appear in additional brackets for certain songs. On disc 1, tracks 4 & 5 have three kanji enclosed in brackets ([]) at the end. No site seems to list these kanji. Can someone tell me what kanji they are (or point me towards a site which actually lists them)? Like this: 04.A-3 ハープ無[***] - Where *** are the three kanji. The same three kanji appear at the end of the following tracks for disc 1: 4, 5, 11, 12, 13, 14. Edit: After some help on KGS, I managed to get the first and last kanji (初録) by having Babelfish translate "First Recording". However, I still don't have the middle kanji. It appears that 取 may be the 2nd kanji. However, the kanji on the scan looks slightly different, with a few less lines on the left-most part of the kanji (I know nothing about kanji! :P). So it is a similar kanji? Yay: Found it! Thank you Wikipedia! 初収録 (Babelfish translates this as "Beginning Record")! [URL=http://img195.imageshack.us/i/2ndv.jpg/] [/URL]

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Wed Aug 19, 2009 11:20 am The kanji triplet means that the songs are being released to album for the first time. "Newly released" or something would do the job, but I'm not sure why you'd include that in your track titles, any more than you would include the other songs' debut albums ("TV-I", etc.). You know, I'm probably going to have to scan the straight track listings after all. Blurgggh.

[]:             Quote Post by FAII » Wed Aug 19, 2009 5:38 pm Reichu wrote: The kanji triplet means that the songs are being released to album for the first time. "Newly released" or something would do the job, but I'm not sure why you'd include that in your track titles, any more than you would include the other songs' debut albums ("TV-I", etc.). You know, I'm probably going to have to scan the straight track listings after all. Blurgggh.

[]:             Quote Post by thedarkorb » Fri Aug 28, 2009 1:27 pm FAII: Just thought I would give the 411 on how I am doing this. I am also very peculiar on how my tracks are titled. Left off things such as A-12 or C-14, which would be listed under the comments field if I had to put them somewhere. They are just numbers used by Shiro before Anno titled them. Here is how I have labeled Disc 4. 01. A Moment When Tension Breaks 02. A Moment When Tension Breaks [Rhythm] 03. A Moment When Tension Breaks [Guitar/Piano] 04. A Moment When Tension Breaks [Fast] 05. A Moment When Tension Breaks [Fast/Rhythm] 06. A Moment When Tension Breaks [Fast/Guitar/Piano] 07. Fly Me to the Moon [A-Type] 08. Fly Me to the Moon [B-Type] 09. Fly Me to the Moon [C-Type] 10. Ritsuko [A-Type] 11. Ritsuko [B-Type] 12. Ritsuko [C-Type] 13. Ritsuko [D-Type] (Three of Me, One of Someone Else) 14. Ritsuko [E-Type] 15. Ritsuko [F-Type] 16. E-12 17. E-12 [Rhythm] 18. Thanatos 19. Thanatos [Rhythm] 20. E-14 21. E-14 [Rhythm] 22. Magmadiver 23. Magmadiver [Rhythm] 24. Magmadiver [Fast] 25. Magmadiver [Fast/Rhythm] 26. The Beast II 27. The Beast II [Rhythm] 28. The Beast II [Fast] (Normal Blood) 29. The Beast II [Fast/Rhythm]

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Sat Aug 29, 2009 12:14 am thedarkorb wrote: They are just numbers used by Shiro before Anno titled them.
